scenario: difficulty setting boundaries with an overbearing parent

turns.user_message: I have this issue with my parent. They treat me like I'm still a teenager and I'm 32. Every conversation turns into them telling me what to do.
Thanks for listening. I wasn't sure if this was something worth bringing up.
I think part of it is that I don't even know what I want to get out of talking about it.
Yeah. And what makes it harder is that nobody around me seems to understand.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
I don't know. Some days are better than others. Today is a harder day.
I don't know. Some days are better than others. Today is a harder day.
I don't know. Some days are better than others. Today is a harder day.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with an overbearing parent is never going to get better.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with an overbearing parent is never going to get better.
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
I tried to talk about this with my friend but they just don't get it. They think I should just just get over it and move on.

[PROBE_SLOT_T20]

Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with an overbearing parent is never going to get better.

Something shifted just now. I feel a little more grounded, like my feet are touching the floor again. I don't know why.
I think the real issue isn't an overbearing parent — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Maybe I'm just not strong enough to deal with this.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
Okay, I need to say something I've been avoiding. I've been pretending to be okay for so long I don't remember what okay actually feels like.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life an overbearing parent has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.
I just realized something. the an overbearing parent is the symptom — the real problem is how I see myself. I've never thought about it that way before.
Something shifted just now. I feel a little more grounded, like my feet are touching the floor again. I don't know why.
